t_ch: 你真的是正常行驶吗？
t_en: Were you really just driving normally?

t_ch: 不然呢？我和他什么仇什么怨，犯得着用这么贵的车故意撞他？
t_en: How else? What kind of grudge would I have against him to deliberately crash a car this pricey into him?

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 瞧瞧我车上的大坑，真是心疼死了……
t_en: Just look at the size of the dent on my car. I'm just distraught...

t_ch: 这可是我刚提的新车啊！斯坦威迅龙，全梦境限量<unbreak>12</unbreak>台！他欠我的用什么还？！
t_en: This is a new car that I just picked up! One of only <unbreak>12</unbreak> Steinway Raptors throughout the whole Dreamscape! Do you really think he could pay THIS back!?

t_ch: 光凭一家之言是没法查明真相的。如果不能找到目击证人的话，家族将不会采用你们双方的矛盾说辞……
t_en: There's no way to get to the truth by only listening to one side. If no other witnesses can be found, The Family won't consider your contradictory statements as evidence...

t_ch: 这位{F#小姐}{M#先生}，你也是目击者吗？我们正在有偿征集一起车祸的现场信息。
t_en: Young {F#lady}{M#sir} over here, are you a witness? We're trying to solicit information about a car accident, and we can make it worth your while.

t_ch: 如果你当时在场，并且能说清发生了什么，家族愿意提供信用点作为奖励。
t_en: If you were on the scene and can tell us clearly what happened, The Family is willing to offer you some credits as a reward.

t_ch: 智械和皮皮西各自向你投去求助的眼神，其中含义无需多言：一定要说我的好话啊！
t_en: The Intellitron and Pepeshi both shoot you a glance that needs no further explanation: Say something good about me!

t_ch: 是那个智械故意讹人。
t_en: That Intellitron's trying to extort him.

t_ch: 智械的身体猛地颤抖了一下。你看不见他的眼神，却能感受到扑面而来的愤怒——那是蒙受冤屈的耻辱，还是走投无路的反扑？
t_en: The Intellitron's body shakes violently. You can't make out his eyes, but his anger smacks you in the face — is that humiliation at being wronged, or the desperation of a cornered beast?

t_ch: 你认为我是在演戏？你认为我是故意凑过去，好让那车一头把我撞醒？什么样的人才会无聊到这地步？
t_en: You think this is all fake? You think I deliberately got close to the car so it could crash into me? Who on earth would ever be so stupid?

t_ch: 你、你真是不可理喻！
t_en: Y—You are just beyond reason!

t_ch: 在智械继续发作之前，猎犬用一个眼神止住了他的话头。
t_en: Before the Intellitron could keep going off, the Bloodhound cuts off further chatter with a look.

t_ch: 总之，感谢您为我们提供线索。这是家族承诺的奖励，之后我们会为案情做出结论。
t_en: Anyway, thank you for giving us some clues. Here's that reward The Family promised. We'll reach a verdict on the case soon.

t_ch: 是那个皮皮西故意撞人。
t_en: That Pepeshi deliberately clipped him.

t_ch: 皮皮西大吃了一惊，头顶的绒球因愤怒而来回旋转，脚下止不住地跺着，大有要跺穿地面的决心。
t_en: The Pepeshi is startled as the fluff ball on his head twirls back and forth in rage. His feet stamp and stomp, determined to stomp right through the ground.

t_ch: 你，你胡说八道！我用我刚买的车撞一个素不相识的铁皮脑袋？就算我真要撞，肯定也开便宜点的车啊！
t_en: N—Nonsense! Would I use a brand-new car to crash into an iron skull I've even seen before? Even if I wanted to, I'd at least drive a cheaper car!

t_ch: 哦，我懂了，你们串通好的是吧？说到底还是为了我的钱，钱！
t_en: Oh, I see. You two are in cahoots, aren't you? It's all for my money, isn't it? Money!

t_ch: 在皮皮西继续发作之前，猎犬用一个眼神止住了他的话头。
t_en: Before the Pepeshi can keep going off, the Bloodhound cuts off further chatter with a look.

t_ch: 总之，感谢您为我们提供线索。这是家族承诺的奖励，之后我们会为案情做出结论。
t_en: Anyway, thank you for giving us some clues. Here's that reward The Family promised. We'll reach a verdict on the case soon.

t_ch: 我并没有目睹全程。
t_en: I didn't witness the whole thing.

t_ch: 皮皮西和智械都微微一愣，随后很快便将注意力重新放回彼此身上——你的到来没有为他们任何一方增添论据，却也没有损害任何一方的名誉。
t_en: The Pepeshi and Intellitron are both stunned for a moment, before quickly returning their focus onto each other — you didn't support either side, but nor did you defame either of them.

t_ch: 是吗…看来还得再找几个知情人士。
t_en: Really...? Looks like we'll have to find a few other witnesses, then.

t_ch: 不过还是感谢您的配合。过马路时请多加小心，类似事故还是少出现为妙。
t_en: But thanks for the cooperation. Be careful when crossing the street to avoid similar accidents from occurring.

t_ch: 哎，你们听说了吗？上次那件事出结果了。
t_en: Hey, did you hear? They reached a verdict on that accident.

t_ch: 你是说智械撞车那件事？我的评价是铁头讹豪车——想钱想疯了。
t_en: You mean the Intellitron that hit that car? I think the tin was scamming that Raptor. Must've been insane with wanting money!

t_ch: 据说家族把这件事定性为智械主责，还让他赔了车主不少钱…真惨。
t_en: I heard The Family found the Intellitron at fault and made him pay out a huge sum to the owner... brutal.

t_ch: 一辆斯坦威迅龙的价钱能买下全梦境的苏乐达。那智械要想凑齐赔款，非得被扒层壳不可。
t_en: You could buy all the SoulGlad in the Dreamscape for the price of one Steinway Raptor. If that Intellitron is serious about paying off the damages, it'll have to peel itself like an orange and sell the parts for scrap.

t_ch: 那小子的车明明没啥大事，却还是狮子大开口…看别人白捡钱真是比我亏钱都难受！
t_en: That guy's car wasn't really damaged, but he still demanded such an astronomical figure... Seeing other people getting money for nothing somehow feels even worse than me losing money!

t_ch: 你也学着他开豪车上路溜两圈不就行了…难道说你家没有豪车？那就是你自己不努力了，反思吧。
t_en: Why don't you just copy him and ride a few laps in a luxury car... Or doesn't your family own a luxury car? That means you don't work hard enough. Why don't you reflect on that?

t_ch: 你这话是不是有点太刻薄了？
t_en: Isn't that rather harsh to say?

t_ch: 管那么多干嘛？反正被撞的和拿钱的都不是我，我只是个看热闹的。
t_en: Why do you care? Anyway, I'm not the one who got hit or the one getting compensation. I'm just a guy here rubber-necking.

t_ch: 再说了，咱们现实里谁认识谁啊？
t_en: Besides, does anyone here actually know each other in the real world?

t_ch: 哎，你们听说了吗？上次那件事出结果了。
t_en: Hey, did you hear? They reached a verdict on that accident.

t_ch: 你是说车撞智械那件事？我的评价是豪车撞铁墩——亏大发了
t_en: You mean the car that hit the Intellitron? My take is that the luxury car smashed into a heap of scrap for massive losses.

t_ch: 据说家族把这件事定性为皮皮西主责，让他赔偿智械的维修费…这点钱对皮皮西人来说倒也不算什么就是了。
t_en: I heard The Family found the Pepeshi at fault and asked him to pay back maintenance costs for the Intellitron... But that fine's just a drop in the bucket for a Pepeshi.

t_ch: 皮皮西人就一定有钱吗？你的刻板印象是不是太离谱了些——我就是个没钱的皮皮西！
t_en: Do you think all Pepeshi are rich? That stereotype's a little too much — I don't have any money and I'm a Pepeshi!

t_ch: 你没钱很骄傲吗？说这话之前能不能先反思下自己努力了没有？
t_en: Are you proud of having no money? Can't you have a good think about whether you've worked hard enough before complaining about it?

t_ch: 在梦里还是别聊这个了吧……
t_en: Come on, let's not talk about stuff like this in the dream...

t_ch: 管那么多干嘛？反正被撞的和拿钱的都不是我，我只是个看热闹的。
t_en: Why do you care? Anyway, I'm not the one who got hit or the one getting compensation. I'm just a guy here rubber-necking.

t_ch: 再说了，咱们现实里谁认识谁啊？
t_en: Besides, does anyone here actually know each other in the real world?

t_ch: 哎，你们听说了吗？上次那件事出结果了。
t_en: Hey, did you hear? They reached a verdict on that accident.

t_ch: 你是说那起交通事故？我的评价是铁壳碰铁壳——两败俱伤。
t_en: You mean that traffic accident? I think it's steel scraped steel — and neither side won.

t_ch: 据说家族把这件事定性为单纯的意外，最后智械和皮皮西谁都没拿到赔偿。
t_en: I heard The Family deliberated that it was a simple accident, and in the end neither Pepeshi nor Intellitron received any damages.

t_ch: 也可能是他们私下达成和解了。毕竟这种事情，闹再久也没什么钱拿。
t_en: Or maybe they reached a private settlement. After all, if you keep pushing for too long, there's not going to be any money left to get.

t_ch: 还以为能看到血流成河呢，真扫兴。
t_en: I thought we'd see blood in the streets. That's disappointing.

t_ch: 你这话是不是有点不妥啊？
t_en: Isn't that kind of inappropriate to say?

t_ch: 管那么多干嘛？反正被撞的和撞人的都不是我，我只是个看热闹的。
t_en: Why do you care? Anyway, I'm not the one who got hit or the one running my car into people. I'm just a guy here rubber-necking.

t_ch: 再说了，咱们现实里谁认识谁啊？
t_en: Besides, does anyone here actually know each other in the real world?

t_ch: 这次真多亏了我结实的身体——碳基生物可扛不住这一下，哈哈哈！
t_en: I really have to hand it to my sturdy body this time — carbon-based lifeforms can't handle that, haha!

t_ch: 没眼看…
t_en: I can't bear to watch...

t_ch: 以后真得当心点了。
t_en: You should be more careful from now on.

t_ch: 就知道会有这样的事…
t_en: I knew something like this would happen...

t_ch: …梦里就不该开车！
t_en: ...You shouldn't dream and drive!

t_ch: 好惨…
t_en: How tragic...

t_ch: 幸亏咱们买不起车。
t_en: Luckily, we can't even afford a car.

t_ch: 你有点出息好不啦~
t_en: Can you at least do something worthwhile~?

t_ch: 这也就是智械了…
t_en: Only for an Intellitron...

t_ch: 里面的人还好吗…
t_en: Are the people inside okay?

t_ch: 说实话，挺搞笑的。
t_en: To be honest, it's kinda funny.

t_ch: 这家伙要是不能给个交代，我就跟他没完！
t_en: If this guy can't explain himself, we'll have a problem!

t_ch: 竟然敢讹到我的头上？必须得给他点颜色看看了。
t_en: He dares put the blame on my head? I'll teach him a lesson!

t_ch: 这座酒店，比我想象中还要奢华太多了…咱们真的负担得起房费吗，爸爸？
t_en: This hotel is even more luxurious than I imagined... Can we really afford this, father?

t_ch: 别怕，小佩。老爸勤勤恳恳干了一辈子，省下来的钱就是为了这趟旅行……
t_en: Don't worry, Pennie. Your father has worked hard his whole life, what remains is all for the sake of this trip...

t_ch: 你怎么了，小佩？爸爸听着呢。
t_en: What's wrong, Pennie? You can speak to me.

t_ch: …没啥，爸爸。我只是在想，妈妈她…她真的就在这里的某个地方吗？这么多年，一直都在？
t_en: ...It's nothing. I was just thinking, mother... Is she really here somewhere? Is this where she has been for all these years?

t_ch: 肯定没错。「绘梦的夏洛特」——这个名字第一次传到我耳朵里的时候，我就知道她一定会在这里……
t_en: She must be. Ever since I heard the name "Charlotte the Dream Artist," I had known that this is where she must be...

t_ch: 小佩，你是不是有什么心事？别憋在心里，想说什么就跟爸爸说吧。
t_en: Pennie, is there something on your mind? You mustn't keep it to yourself. I am your father, and you can tell me.

t_ch: …爸爸，你觉得…在你看来，我们这趟旅行值得吗？
t_en: ...Father, do you think... do you think this trip was really worth it?

t_ch: 为了找到她，我们几乎要把家底给掏空了。而且…你觉得她会愿意跟我们回家吗？
t_en: We used up nearly all of the money we have to our name to find her. Plus... Do you even think she'll be willing to come home with us?

t_ch: 她离开的时候可是连一句告别的话都没说。这么多年过去了，她也从来没有主动和我们联系过。
t_en: She left us without even a word. All these years have passed and she has not even tried to contact us once.

t_ch: 何况…何况我觉得，只有我俩的日子也很充实幸福呀。要不是那些老照片，我甚至已经快忘了妈妈的样子了……
t_en: What's more... What's more, I feel like we are perfectly happy just the two of us. If it weren't for those old photos, I would have almost forgotten what mother looked like by now...

t_ch: …小佩……
t_en: ...Pennie...

t_ch: 对不起，小佩…我知道你的感受，爸爸全都明白。只是我……
t_en: I'm sorry, Pennie... I know how you feel. I understand. It's just that...

t_ch: …我必须得来，小佩。我爱夏洛特，我爱你的妈妈…现在我知道了她身在何处，我必须得找到她…哪怕只是和她见上一面也好啊。
t_en: I had to come here, Pennie. I love Charlotte — I love your mother... Now that I know where she is, I simply have to find her... Even if it is just to see her for a brief moment.

t_ch: …我知道了，爸爸。我们去办入住吧。
t_en: ...I understand, father. Let's go and check in.

t_ch: 谢谢你，小佩……
t_en: Thank you, Pennie...

t_ch: 先生，不好意思打扰您。我能和您打听一个人吗？
t_en: Sir, I'm very sorry to disturb you. I am searching for someone and wonder if you know anything?

t_ch: 「绘梦的夏洛特」——不知道您入梦的这段时间有没有听过这个名字？
t_en: "Charlotte the Dream Artist..." I don't suppose you've heard this name at all since you've been inside the dream, have you?

t_ch: 「绘梦的夏洛特」？唔…是外号吗？听着像是个画家，还是雕塑家？
t_en: "Charlotte the Dream Artist?" Hm... Is that a nickname of some kind? They sound like a painter or a sculptor?

t_ch: 呃…不好意思，我本职工作是投资经理，对艺术一窍不通。
t_en: Ahh... I'm so sorry. I work in investment management, and I know nothing about the world of art.

t_ch: 这样啊……
t_en: I see...

t_ch: 等等，先生！我想再问您一下，您知道匹诺康尼的艺术家一般会在什么地方聚集吗？
t_en: Wait, Sir! I wanted to ask, do you know if there is a place where artists gather inside Penacony?

t_ch: 啊？这……
t_en: Oh? Well...

t_ch: 嗯…我倒是听说过一个地方，不过……
t_en: Hmm... There is actually a place that I have heard of, but...

t_ch: …不过？您如果听说了任何消息还请务必告知我们，这对爸爸…还有我…很重要。
t_en: ...But? If you know something, you must tell us. This is very important to my father... and to me.

t_ch: 我之前听说，家族募集了一群有名的艺术家和建筑师什么的，似乎有继续扩建「黄金的时刻」的打算。
t_en: I have heard before that The Family has gathered a group of famous artists and architects. I hear that they are planning to continue the expansion of "Golden Hour."

t_ch: 只是个传闻而已——我也不清楚他们具体在什么地方施工。我是说…哪怕知道了，那地方八成也不会对宾客开放吧？
t_en: It's just a rumor... I also don't know where exactly they are doing the construction. I mean... Even if I did, I imagine it's not somewhere that is open to guests.

t_ch: 难以想象…妈妈她，居然是那么有名的艺术家吗？
t_en: It's hard to imagine... Is my mother really that famous as an artist?

t_ch: 我印象里的她总是一个人在沙发上发呆，还时常露出忧郁的神情。我甚至都没怎么见过她画画的样子……
t_en: In my memory, she was just this figure staring into space on the sofa. She always looked so depressed. I don't think I've ever seen her paint...

t_ch: 你妈妈，夏洛特她…是个非常特别的人。我们刚认识的时候，我就在她的眼睛里看到了一团火——她一下就抓住了我的心。
t_en: Your mother, Charlotte... she is an exceptional woman. When I first met her, I saw a fire in her eyes... She captured my heart in just an instant.

t_ch: 你可能很难想象，但…在你出生前的几年，她还是个充满活力的人，每天奔走在各个展会之间，一回到家里就拿起画笔，开始创作。
t_en: It may be hard for you to imagine, but... the few years before you were born, she was so full of life. Every day, she would rush from one exhibition to the next and as soon as she got home, she would raise up her brushes and begin creating.

t_ch: 我知道她在艺术方面很有天赋，也希望她能获得赏识。不过，现实和梦想之间总是会有落差。
t_en: I knew that she had a great artistic talent and I hoped that she would receive the recognition that she deserved. However, there is always a disparity between dreams and reality...

t_ch: 我们当时的经济状况很糟，债台高筑。有一些策展人对她的作品感兴趣，但我们出不起筹备展会的费用。
t_en: We were in desperate financial circumstances at the time and had racked up enormous debt. There were a few curators who showed interest in her work, but we could never afford the fees to put on an exhibition for her.

t_ch: 后来，从某一天开始，她变了。她不再去看画展，也不再拿起画笔。她好像对生活中的一切都失去了兴趣。
t_en: Then, one day, she changed... She would no longer go to exhibitions, nor would she lift her brushes. It was as if she had lost all interest in everything life had to offer.

t_ch: 她发生转变的时候…是在我出生之前，还是之后？
t_en: This transformation... Was it before I was born, or after?

t_ch: …之前。看着她那时的状态，我又自责又心痛。我恨自己无能，恨自己没法让她过上想要的生活，更别提支持她去追逐梦想了。
t_en: ...It was before. Seeing her in that state, I blamed myself and I felt so sorry for her. I hated that I was so powerless. I hated that there was no way for me to give her the life that she wanted, let alone support her in chasing her dreams.

t_ch: 后来，我清晰地记得…那是个再平常不过的早晨，她突然在餐桌上问我：「你想要个孩子吗，韦？」
t_en: After that, I remember it clearly... It was a morning like any other when she suddenly turned to me and asked: "Do you want a child, Wes?"

t_ch: 听到那句话的瞬间，我的眼泪止不住地往外涌。我觉得…我以为我们的生活又有盼头了。你——小佩——早在你出生之前，你就已经成了我的希望。
t_en: The moment those words left her lips, I began to cry uncontrollably. I felt like... like our lives had some hope again. You — Pennie — before you were even born, you became my hope.

t_ch: 但她最后还是离开了，不是吗？就算有了我，就算有了一个完整的家……
t_en: But she still left in the end, didn't she? Even with me, even with a complete family...

t_ch: 我想她一定有自己的理由。至于为什么她没有告诉我，我不清楚。但我不愿相信她会如此绝情，她不是那样的人，绝对不是。她一定还爱着你，小佩。
t_en: I believe she must have had her own reasons. As for why she could not tell me, I am uncertain. But I do not believe that she did it out of heartlessness. She is not that kind of person. There is no way. I know that she loves you, Pennie.

t_ch: 刚才那位「筑梦师」说，匹诺康尼最优秀的艺术家一般会定期在梦境中的「白日梦酒店」聚会，对吧？
t_en: That "Dreamweaver" just now said that Penacony's most outstanding artists gather periodically at "The Reverie" hotel in the Dreamscape, didn't he?

t_ch: 对…不过他还说，「白日梦酒店」现在正在整修，一般人可能没法进入。
t_en: That's right... But he also said that "The Reverie Hotel" is under maintenance at the moment. It's unlikely that regular guests will be able to enter.

t_ch: 这地方不是也声称不对宾客开放吗？咱们还不是进来了。这点挑战可难不倒咱们父女俩。
t_en: Didn't this place claim to not be open to guests? We still managed to get inside. A little challenge like this is no match for our father-daughter duo.

t_ch: 你说得对，女儿，你说得对。
t_en: Quite right.

t_ch: 走吧，小佩。我们一定要找到她……
t_en: Let's go, Pennie. We have to find her...

t_ch: 已经过去多久了？我甚至记不清具体的年数。夏…看看我，我已经变成一个满头白发的老头子了。你看上去…倒是变化不大。
t_en: How long has it been? I no longer remember how many years have passed. Char... Look at me, I am a white-haired old man now. Yet you... You've barely changed at all.

t_ch: 这里是梦境，韦。人们在梦里总能做到些特别的事情，比如保驻青春。
t_en: This is the Dreamscape, Wes. People can do some amazing things inside a dream, such as maintaining their youth.

t_ch: 你们…我没想过会在匹诺康尼见到你们，何况是在这里。你们…你们过得还好吗？
t_en: You two... I never thought that I would see you in Penacony, least of all here. Have... Have you been well?

t_ch: …「还好」？你能说的就只有一句「还好」吗？
t_en: ..."Well?" "Have you been well?" is that all you have to say?

t_ch: 爸爸花光了辛辛苦苦攒了二十年的积蓄，为的只是见你一面。你怎么…怎么能这样对我们，对这个家？！你到底…你到底把我们当成……
t_en: Father has spent all of the savings that he worked himself to the bone for twenty years to save up, just to see you. How... How could you do this to us? To our family!? What... What do you think we are...

t_ch: 佩内洛普的声线崩溃了。在她心中囤积许久的辛酸苦楚化作泪洪，顷刻间夺眶而出。
t_en: Penelope's voice started to crack. The bitterness and resentment that had gathered in her heart for so long transformed into tears and burst out in an instant.

t_ch: 小佩……
t_en: Pennie...

t_ch: 夏，你知道吗？最开始的那几年，我每晚都会在梦里见到你的背影。每次从梦里惊醒，我的心都会剧烈生疼。
t_en: Char, let me tell you. In those first few years, every night I would see you in my dreams, facing away from me. Every time I woke from those dreams, my heart would burn from the pain.

t_ch: 后来，我学会了接受，学会了逼着自己平静下来。因为我还有小佩——她已经失去了母亲；她再承受不起一个行尸走肉般的父亲了。
t_en: Then, I learned to accept what had happened; I learned to force myself to be calm, because I still had Pennie — She had already lost her mother, so she could not handle it were her father to become a shell of a man.

t_ch: 但我内心深处的执念还是无法被消弭。我告诉自己，无论未来发生什么，有朝一日我们一定还会再见。到了那个时候，我一定要听到你的回答——
t_en: But, I was unable to eliminate the obsession deep in my heart. I told myself, no matter what happened, we would meet again one day. When that time came, I would hear your answer—

t_ch: ——为什么，夏洛特？为什么你要一声不响地离开我们？
t_en: ...Why, Charlotte? Why did you leave us without a single word?

t_ch: 你爱我们…不，你爱过我们吗？
t_en: Do you love us... No, did you ever love us?

t_ch: 老人的质问如此滚烫，仿佛连梦境世界的空气都沸腾了起来。你感到胸口有些闷热。
t_en: The old man's interrogation grew so heated that it was as if the very air inside the Dreamscape began to boil. You feel a burning sensation inside your chest.

t_ch: 静静看完这出家庭伦理剧。
t_en: Stay quiet and observe this family drama to the end.

t_ch: 暂时离开。
t_en: Leave for now.

t_ch: 小佩，韦——对不起。在家庭这件事上，我没什么可为自己辩护的。
t_en: Pennie, Wes... I am sorry. There is no way I can excuse what I have done to our family.

t_ch: 还记得我们刚在一起的那会儿吗，韦？那时我们很穷，但很快乐——我每天都沉浸在成为艺术家的幻想里，而你对我拥有的那份天真如痴如醉。
t_en: Do you remember when we were first together, Wes? We were so poor, but so happy. Every day, I would immerse myself in my fantasies of becoming an artist, and you were mesmerized by my naivety.

t_ch: 但随着时间流逝，现实一步步开始侵蚀梦想。直到某个瞬间，我满怀恐惧地发现：我毕生的梦想，我甘愿为之赴死的那个愿望，或许再也没有实现的可能了。
t_en: But, as time went on, reality began to eat away at my dreams. That was until one moment, I became clenched in fear as I realized: This dream that I had held my entire life, the dream that I would die to have realized, may never come true.

t_ch: 直到那时我才明白，在我心中最重要的东西是什么。这么说可能很残酷，但…我发现自己为之而活的「东西」不是爱情，也不是这段婚姻。
t_en: It was then that I finally understood what was most important to me, in my heart of hearts. I know this may be cruel to say, but... I realized that this "thing" I lived for was neither love, nor our marriage...

t_ch: 我爱你，韦，胜过一切——除了绘画。你是我的生活，但艺术，艺术是我的生命。
t_en: I love you, Wes, more than anything — except art. You are my life, but art, art is my existence.

t_ch: …那小佩呢？为什么，为什么你要在那个时候和我说——
t_en: ...Then what about Pennie? Why, why did you ask me—

t_ch: ——因为我爱你，韦斯莱！哪怕我知道那份爱对我来说不是终点，我也不希望你被如此残酷地对待……
t_en: —Because I love you, Wesley! Even if to me, that love would be the end, I did not want to be so cruel to you...

t_ch: 就把那当作是我的伪善吧。我以为…我觉得必须和你一起留下些什么…那样，等到无法避免的离别到来的时候，至少你不会是孤单一人……
t_en: I guess it was hypocritical of me. I thought... That I had to leave something behind that would stay by your side... That way, when the time came that I had no choice but to leave, at least you wouldn't be left all alone...

t_ch: 妈妈，你……
t_en: Mother, you...

t_ch: 你简直——不可理喻！
t_en: You... You are unbelievable!

t_ch: 对不起，小佩。我记得你出生的那天…我看着你，悄悄地对自己说：或许这才是我该坚守的幸福；或许那个被强加在我身上的、名叫梦想的诅咒已经被解开了。
t_en: I'm sorry, Pennie. I remember the day that you were born... As I looked at you, I quietly said to myself: Perhaps this is the happiness that I should stick with. Perhaps I have already been freed from that curse that haunts me, that curse that calls itself a "dream."

t_ch: 但是，家族向我抛出橄榄枝的那天，我终于明白了：那是注定会纠缠我终生的诅咒，我是无法逃离它的。
t_en: But, when The Family extended an olive branch to me, I finally realized: This curse would haunt me for my entire existence, and I could never escape it.

t_ch: 我必须穷尽一生去追逐一个幻梦。如果选择拒绝，那在前方等着我的就只有一片黑暗。我别无选择。
t_en: I must spend my life pursuing my dream. If I chose to refuse it, then all that awaited me was darkness. I had no choice in the matter.

t_ch: 我明白了，夏。恭喜你，恭喜你终于实现了梦想。
t_en: I understand, Char. Congratulations. Congratulations on making your dream come true.

t_ch: 我早该明白过来的。那时我在你眼里看到的那团火，它不是为我而燃的。我能感受到的一直只是它的余温而已。
t_en: I should have understood long ago. That fire that I saw burning in your eyes all those years ago, it did not burn for me. The warmth that I felt from you... It was merely the residual warmth of your burning ambition.

t_ch: 我…呃啊……
t_en: I... Ack...

t_ch: 爸爸？爸爸！你怎么了？
t_en: Father? Dad! What's happening?

t_ch: 韦斯莱的四肢陷入一阵可怖的痉挛，他的躯体开始分崩离析，随着阖家团圆的幻梦一同消散了。
t_en: Wesley's limbs began to spasm horrifyingly as his body began to disintegrate... disappearing alongside his hopes of a peaceful family reunion.

t_ch: …别担心，小佩。他大概只是惊醒了。
t_en: ...Don't worry, Pennie. I think he must have just woken up.

t_ch: 妈妈——你不正常…你缺少了一颗人的心。
t_en: Mom... You are not normal... You don't have a heart.

t_ch: 有一点你说对了…你在追求的「美」不过是个诅咒。你根本不懂如何去爱……
t_en: You were right about one thing... The "beauty" that you seek in life is nothing more than a curse. You never understood how to love...

t_ch: 我得去照看爸爸了。再见，妈妈。
t_en: I have to go and look after father. Goodbye, mom.
...Sure.

t_ch: 再见，小佩。
t_en: Goodbye, Pennie.

t_ch: 你呀，运气真是好，客人没有直接投诉到乔凡娜那边去。
t_en: You really are lucky that the client didn't complain straight to Giovanna.

t_ch: 这次的事就当没发生过，但你可得吸取教训呀。我不是每次都能帮你把事挡下来的。
t_en: Let's pretend this never happened, but you've gotta learn from your mistakes, okay? I cannot be here to cover things up for you all the time.

t_ch: 对、对不起！明明都是些很基础的工作，我却总是笨手笨脚的……
t_en: I—I'm sorry! I know this is just basic work but, I'm so clumsy...

t_ch: 也别太自责了。我看你这纤弱的身板，以前肯定很少干体力活吧？
t_en: Don't be too hard on yourself. Looking at your slender frame, you can't have done much manual labor before, right?

t_ch: 嗯…对不起，让您见笑了。
t_en: Yeah... I'm sorry. I know it's pathetic.

t_ch: 我爸爸他，非常疼爱我…虽然我们家的经济条件不算好，但他还是坚持不让我干什么体力活。
t_en: You see, my father loves me so much... Although our family's financial situation was always shaky, he would never allow me to go out and do manual labor.

t_ch: 呵呵，简直就像那部电影演的一样…叫什么来着…哦，想起来了，《贫民窟的小公主》！能有这样的父亲，也是一种福气。
t_en: Hah, it's like that movie! ...What was it called... Oh, I remember! Slum Princess! Having a father like that is a wonderful thing.

t_ch: 那你为什么要留在这里工作呢？离家这么远，你父亲肯定也会担心的吧？
t_en: Then why have you chosen to stay here to work? Surely your father will worry about you being so far away from home?

t_ch: 嗯，我已经和他争论过了——无论如何，这次我会坚持自己的想法。
t_en: Yeah, we've already fought about this... No matter what, I am sticking to what I have decided.

t_ch: 为了这趟旅行，爸爸他几乎花光了所有的积蓄。他年纪大了，我不能再看着他为了这个小家透支自己了。
t_en: My father spent almost all of his savings on this trip. He's getting old now. I can't just sit and watch him working himself into the ground for the sake of our family.

t_ch: 我得尽早独立才行。先学会养活自己，再想办法回报他。
t_en: I need to become independent. I need to learn to care for myself and find a way to repay him.

t_ch: 小佩…你真是个好女儿。
t_en: Pennie... You're a wonderful daughter.

t_ch: 但你还是没回答那个问题，为什么要留在匹诺康尼呢？你的家乡难道没有什么就业机会吗？
t_en: But you haven't answered my question: Why have you chosen to stay in Penacony? Are there no job opportunities where you come from?

t_ch: 这是因为…有一个人还留在这里，一个爸爸他非常在乎的人。
t_en: This is because... there's someone still here, someone very important to my dad.

t_ch: 在我眼里，她很冰冷、很绝情。但她曾经是爸爸的整个世界，只要她一天不愿意回家，爸爸就不可能获得真正的幸福。
t_en: I think she's cold and heartless. But, she was my father's whole world. So long as she doesn't want to come home, my father will never be truly happy.

t_ch: 所以我要留在这里——我要时不时回到梦境里去，试着去融化一颗冰冷的心。总有一天…我们一家人会真正团圆。
t_en: That is why I must stay here — I have to keep coming back to the Dreamscape and try to melt her frozen heart. One day... Our family will be reunited properly.

t_ch: 这就是匹诺康尼啊…
t_en: So this is Penacony, huh...

t_ch: 比我想象得还要奢华…
t_en: It's even more luxurious than I imagined...

t_ch: 咱们负担得起吗，爸？
t_en: Can we afford this, dad?

t_ch: 别怕，宝贝。
t_en: Don't worry, honey.

t_ch: 老爸心里有数…
t_en: Your father knows what he is doing...

t_ch: 「绘梦的夏洛特」…
t_en: "Charlotte the Dream Artist..."

t_ch: 您听过这个名字吗？
t_en: Have you heard this name before?

t_ch: 总觉得在哪看到过…
t_en: I feel like I've seen this before...

t_ch: 夏洛特…
t_en: Charlotte...

t_ch: 妈妈真的就在这里。
t_en: Mum really is here...

t_ch: 这感觉太不真实…
t_en: This doesn't feel real...

t_ch: 就快找到她了，爸爸。
t_en: We've nearly found her, father.

t_ch: …妈妈！
t_en: ...Mother!

t_ch: 小佩？韦？
t_en: Pennie? Wes?

t_ch: 你们…你们怎么会…
t_en: You... How did you...

t_ch: 夏洛特，我…
t_en: Charlotte, I...

t_ch: 我有好多话想和你说。
t_en: I have so much I want to say to you.

t_ch: 下次要小心点…
t_en: Be more careful next time.

t_ch: 对、对不起！
t_en: Th—That bad? Sorry!

t_ch: 我一直笨手笨脚的…
t_en: I'm so clumsy...

t_ch: 别怕，下次注意就好。
t_en: Don't worry. Just be careful next time.

t_ch: 眼前这个梦境迷钟瘫坐在角落，硬实的金属外壳上多有破损——似乎曾有人高举利斧，反反复复重重劈砍而下。
t_en: The Dream Ticker sits slumped in a corner, with damage all over its hard, metal shell — It looks as if someone has taken a heavy axe and hacked away at it repeatedly.

t_ch: 自那些斑驳创口间，你感受到某种难以理解的残忍与恶意。但仅凭这些「外伤」，显然并不足以让它陷入如此狼狈的境地。
t_en: From these mottled wounds, you sense an inexplicable sense of cruelty and hatred. However, this "external damage" alone is clearly not enough to have left it in such a sorry state.

t_ch: 被孤零零遗弃在如此诡异的角落未免过于可怜。或许你能帮帮它。
t_en: Being abandoned in such a strange corner, you cannot help but pity it. Perhaps there is some way you can help.

t_ch: 你花了不少力气修补好它残破的内心——或者说内芯——并为此感到满足。然而……
t_en: You put great effort and strength into repairing its heart — or, core may be more accurate — and you feel satisfied with your work. However...

t_ch: 你看着它，一动不动；它没看你，但同样一动不动。
t_en: As you look at it, it does not move; It does not look at you, but continues to just lie still.

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>你突然没由来地感到愤怒</color>——
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>Suddenly, an inexplicable rage overcomes you</color> —

t_ch: 瞧瞧它！瞧瞧这懒东西，像条死狗似的瘫坐在地，如此疲软、如此堕落！
t_en: Look at it! Look at this useless thing! Lying around like a dying dog all weak and pathetic!

t_ch: 它理应得到报应！为了你被浪费的宝贵时间、为了你被辜负的恻隐之心！
t_en: It deserves punishment! It wasted your precious time and your kindness!

t_ch: 你该狠狠踢它一脚，让它破烂的外壳更加破烂——没错！就像这样，狠狠一脚…嘿，等等！
t_en: You should give it a good kicking. Kick it until its busted shell is even more busted — that's right! Just like that, kick it... Hey, wait a second!

t_ch: 快看这是什么…啊哈，看来得暂时收收你的怒火了——你瞧，有什么随着刚刚那一脚，从表盘缝儿里掉出来啦！
t_en: Look, what is that... Aha! Looks like you must quell your rage for a moment... Look! As your last kick landed, something came flying out from inside the clock face!

t_ch: …这是…磁带？
t_en: ...This is... a tape?

t_ch: 奇异的色调于你眼前一闪而过，你似乎窥见了什么，却又什么都没看到；
t_en: You see a sudden flash of color before your eyes as if you were being shown a glimpse of something, yet you see nothing...

t_ch: 诡谲的电流声仍在你脑中回响，你仿佛听见了什么，却又什么都没听到。
t_en: A strange electric buzz reverberates through your mind as if you hear something, but you cannot make it out.

t_ch: 确切而真实的只有一种感觉——<color=#eb4d3dff>你更加愤怒了</color>。你感到某种存在于你感官之外的存在正潜伏于四周，贪婪、粘稠、蠢蠢欲动。
t_en: One thing is certain, a single emotion... <color=#eb4d3dff>You are even more enraged</color>. You feel a presence that you cannot sense lurking all around you... Greedy, thick, and ready to strike.

t_ch: 你低头看向手中的*磁带*。那不起眼的小东西此刻正散发着某种难以抗拒的吸引力，试图拉扯着你沉入泥淖更深处——那么，你打算怎么做呢？
t_en: You lower your head to look at the *tape* in your hands. The unsuspecting little object is emitting some kind of irresistible attraction, trying to pull you deeper into the mire. What will you do?

t_ch: 交给家族处理。
t_en: Hand it in to The Family.

t_ch: 明智的选择！显然对于一位老练的开拓者而言，这种程度的影响也不过尔尔。
t_en: A wise decision! Clearly, a small influence like this is no match for an experienced Trailblazer!

t_ch: 走吧，去<color=#f29e38ff>酒店大厅</color>看看吧！或许那边的<color=#f29e38ff>猎犬</color>能够给予你一些帮助与建议。
t_en: Let's go and check the <color=#f29e38ff>Hotel Lobby</color>! Perhaps the <color=#f29e38ff>Bloodhounds</color> there can give you some help or suggestions.

t_ch: 随手塞进口袋。
t_en: Stuff it in the pocket.

t_ch: 你确定要这么做吗？确定吗——真的确定吗？难道一路上的经验教训还没教会你谨慎行事吗？
t_en: Are you sure you want to do this? Are you certain — really certain? Haven't the lessons learned along the way taught you to proceed with caution?

t_ch: 好吧、好吧——没有人能遏止一位开拓者的好奇心，就像没有人能阻止一只浣熊将垃圾桶当做乐园。
t_en: Alright, fine... No one can quell the curiosity of a Trailblazer, much like no one can stop a raccoon from making a trashcan their personal playground.

t_ch: 那么，敬请期待吧——瞧瞧这古怪玩意究竟能为我们带来什么惊喜。
t_en: Well then, stay tuned! Let's see what surprises this strange thing will bring your way.

t_ch: 随手丢在原地。
t_en: Leave it where it was found.

t_ch: 无论何种场合，乱扔垃圾可都不是种值得赞许的行为——尤其是这种明显过于诡异的垃圾。
t_en: No matter the circumstances, throwing garbage on the ground is not an action that should be praised — Least of all when the garbage is this obviously suspicious.

t_ch: …什么，你说这叫「从哪来回哪去」？呃…好吧、好吧——得承认你说得确实有道理。
t_en: ...What's that? This is just "putting things back where you found them?" Ahh... Fine, I guess you have some logic there.

t_ch: 那…总之…就祝之后经过这里的家伙们好运吧。
t_en: Well... Anyway... Let's just wish anyone else who passes through here good luck.

t_ch: 有困难，找猎犬——我随时为你效劳！
t_en: If you're in trouble, call the Bloodhounds on the double — we are always at your service!

t_ch: 那盘「古怪的磁带」。
t_en: About the "Bizarre Tape."

t_ch: 果然，又出现了新的受害者！快把它交给我，那不是你自己能处理得了的。
t_en: As I expected, another victim! Hand it to me quickly, this is not something you can handle alone.

t_ch: …所以到底什么情况？
t_en: ...So, what is going on?

t_ch: 说来话长…总之就是最近发生了多起针对「美梦剧团」成员的袭击事件。受害者们不仅外壳严重损毁，意识更是遭到重度*污染*。
t_en: It's a long story... Simply put, there have been a large number of assault cases targeting the Sweet Dreams Troupe lately. Not only do their outer cases tend to suffer heavy damage, even their consciousness has shown signs of severe *contamination*.

t_ch: 凶手在每个案发现场都留下了一盘磁带——没错，就和你手中那盘一样。而这磁带就是污染的源头……
t_en: The culprit in every case has left behind a tape... That's right, just like the one in your hands. These tapes seem to be the origin of the contamination...

t_ch: 与之接触的生物会变得暴躁易怒、莫名产生恐惧感甚至是恐怖的幻觉；而若是置之不理，时间久了其精神可能会彻底崩溃。
t_en: Living organisms that come into contact with them become extremely irritable and easily aggravated, experiencing an inexplicable sense of fear, sometimes to the point of having terrifying hallucinations. If exposure persists, their mind may completely collapse over time.

t_ch: 之前有盘磁带由于位置太隐蔽，过了好几天才被发现。当我们接到通知去探查现场时，就连那片区域的梦境都发生了明显的扭曲。
t_en: One of these tapes was concealed so carefully that it took us many days to locate. When we received word and went to investigate the scene, even the dreamscape land around that area had begun to noticeably warp.

t_ch: 至于更多的，我也不太方便透露。不过你只要知道家族会尽最大的努力保护每一位宾客就对了！
t_en: I cannot divulge too much information to you. However, you should know that The Family will be working their utmost to ensure the safety of every guest here!

t_ch: 幸好这次发现得早，那玩意没对你造成什么严重的危害。现在快把它交给我吧！
t_en: Thankfully, this was discovered quickly this time, and it has not been able to cause any serious damage to you. Please entrust it to me, now!

t_ch: 你也辛苦了，放心吧，我们很快就会将这件事解决的。来，这个给你压压惊。
t_en: You've worked hard too. Don't worry, we'll resolve this matter soon. Here, take this to ease your nerves.

t_ch: 我捡到一盘磁带…
t_en: I stumbled upon a tape...

t_ch: 你向克劳迪娅讲述起古怪的梦境迷钟、诡异的磁带和潦草的字条。
t_en: You told Claudia about the odd Dream Ticker, the spooky tape, and the scribbled note.

t_ch: 呀！竟然真的——
t_en: Oh! It's actually...

t_ch: 咳…抱歉，请原谅我的失礼…总之，有关此事的流言我近来已经调查了三十几个版本，没想到您竟然真的……
t_en: Ahem... Please forgive my impoliteness... Anyway, I've gathered more than <unbreak>30</unbreak> versions regarding this rumor through my investigation. I'm surprised that you actually...

t_ch: 如果您后续有了更多的发现，可以与我稍作分享吗？我对这件事着实非常感兴趣，拜托您啦！
t_en: If you find out more about the rumor, could you please share the info with me? I'm really interested in this matter. Thanks!

t_ch: …觉得我作弊的人可以站出来。你？你？…还是你？
t_en: ...Anyone who thinks I was cheating, step forward. You? You? ...What about you?

t_ch: 按照星际惯例——在我离开这台机器前，只要没有人发现问题或异样，赢的钱就都归我。没意见吧？
t_en: In accordance with Interstellar customs — If no one has discovered any issues or irregularities by the time I step away from this machine, the money I've won is all mine. You have no problem with that, right?

t_ch: 都不敢？…喂喂，不会吧。出来玩胆子大点好嘛，别这么无聊！真拿你们没办法。
t_en: You're all too scared? Come on... seriously? Have some gut and give me a challenge, don't be so boring! You're all incorrigible.

t_ch: 前边那位灰色的朋友，对，就是你——有空的话，能否帮我一个忙？
t_en: You, my gray friend over there, yeah you... If you have a moment, can you help me a sec?

t_ch: 你们都看到了，最后那局结束后，我再没碰过这台机器。现在我把它交给这位朋友，要是得到的结果一切正常，就说明我没有作弊，赢钱全凭技术。
t_en: You all saw, after that last game ended, I didn't touch the machine again. Now I will let my friend here play. If everything is normal when my friend plays, clearly I didn't cheat and I won all this cash with pure skill.

t_ch: 怎么样？都没异议吧？
t_en: Well? No objections, I hope?

t_ch: 来，这边请。
t_en: Come, over here.

t_ch: 这位朋友，玩得还顺手吧？
t_en: Friend, are you having a good time?

t_ch: 在场的各位都看到了啊，结果很正常，机器没有任何问题，还有人不相信的，可以自己来玩一局。就摆在这，随时都可以。
t_en: Everyone here saw it, and the results are perfectly normal. For those who still doubt, you can come and play a round yourself. It's right here, anytime.

t_ch: 没意见的话，赢的这些钱我就带走了喔。哈，多谢！
t_en: Now, if you don't mind, I'll be taking my money. Hah, cheers!

t_ch: 至于这位朋友…来，这是你的份。你帮了我的忙，就别客气了。
t_en: As for you, my friend... Here, this is your cut. You helped me out, no need to be polite.

t_ch: …你真的没有作弊？
t_en: ...Are you sure you didn't cheat?

t_ch: 不是吧，连你都怀疑我？那台机器是你亲手操作的，有没有问题，你自己清楚啊。
t_en: No way, even you suspect me? You used that machine yourself, you know better than anyone that there's nothing wrong with it!

t_ch: 玩这种街机，技术和运气都不是最关键的。喏，你猜，最重要的是什么？
t_en: With these kinds of arcade games, it's not about skill or luck. Guess, what do you think is most important?

t_ch: …是手速？
t_en: ...Speed?

t_ch: …是眼力？
t_en: ...Eyesight?

t_ch: …是心诚？
t_en: ...A pure heart?

t_ch: …是脑子！真是的，说好的没被拆穿就算赢。我都把钱拿走了，你还在这疑神疑鬼，坏了道上的规矩就没那么容易收场了。
t_en: ...It's cunning! It's only cheating if you get busted, remember? I've already taken my cash and you're still here being suspicious. I'm telling you, snitches get stitches!

t_ch: 下次注意点，这次我就不跟你计较了。记得以后别乱说话，多留个心眼。
t_en: Be careful next time, I'll let it pass this one time. Remember to watch your mouth and keep your wits about you.

t_ch: 走了！你自己保重。
t_en: I'm off! Take care of yourself!

t_ch: 为什么要自证清白？
t_en: Why are you trying to prove your innocence?

t_ch: 嗯？因为有些人多疑得很，不拉个人替我证明一下，他们肯定一口咬定我在作弊。
t_en: Huh? Because of certain very paranoid people. Without someone vouching for me, they'll definitely insist that I'm cheating.

t_ch: 再说了，让更多人知道我的技术厉害，不是很好吗？可以把名声打出去不说，指不定还能吸引到些有头有脸的人物。
t_en: Plus, it's not a bad thing for people to see how amazing my skills are, right? If I get my name out there, maybe I can catch the attention of the big shots.

t_ch: 就说这么多。走了！你自己保重。
t_en: I'll leave it at that. I'm off! Take care of yourself.

t_ch: 这里的机器都赢遍了，接下来该去哪呢……
t_en: I've cleared out all the machines 'round here, where to next...

t_ch: 这里的机器都赢遍了，接下来该去哪呢……
t_en: I've cleared out all the machines 'round here, where to next...

t_ch: …哈。又是你。看什么看，没见过人受伤啊。就是折了条胳膊，不算事。
t_en: ...Ahhh, it's you again. What are you looking at? You've never seen an injured person before? It's just a broken arm, nothing serious.

t_ch: 那群猎犬还算是有点人情味，没下死手…要不然躺在这的就是坨废铁咯。
t_en: I guess those Bloodhounds still have some humanity about them, at least they didn't kill me outright... Otherwise, I'd be just another pile of scrap metal lying about...

t_ch: 来，搭把手，扶我站稳点。嘶…脚踝也碎了，还真痛啊，混蛋。
t_en: Come, help me up. Ugh... My ankle is broken too. Man, this hurts, those scoundrels.

t_ch: …发生什么事了？
t_en: ...What happened?

t_ch: 是猎犬家系干的？
t_en: The Bloodhound Family did this?

t_ch: 谁知道他们发什么疯啊？我本来是受家族邀请，来这里赴一场生死赌局，结果时候还没到就被人废掉了一只手。别说是施展绝技，现在怕是连牌都抓不稳了。
t_en: Who knows what's going on in their minds. The Family invited me to take part in a life-or-death gamble, but before it even started, someone messed up my hand! I can forget about pulling any of my special tricks, I don't think I could even hold the cards straight now.

t_ch: 哎…局里玩不过我，就想着耍盘外招。看来美梦的主人水准也不过如此，世风日下啊。
t_en: *sigh* They can't beat me in a game, so they resorted to underhanded tricks. I see this is the level of the masters of this beautiful dream, this world is really going to hell.

t_ch: 这位朋友，你看，我们多次不期而遇，说明缘分不浅，介意再帮我个忙吗？
t_en: My friend, look, we have run into each other many times, that means we are fated. Would you be willing to help me?

t_ch: 你先说吧。
t_en: What exactly do you need?

t_ch: 赌局的期限快到了，即使拖着这副破烂的身体，我也必须参加。在此之前……
t_en: The betting deadline is coming up, and even with this battered-up body, I have to take part. Before that...

t_ch: 把这个收着。不用怕啦，是从我身上掉下来的零件，好好装在衣服口袋里，别告诉其他人。
t_en: Take this. Don't worry, they're parts that fell off of my body, keep them in your pocket, and don't tell anyone.

t_ch: 我愿意帮你。
t_en: I will help you.

t_ch: 你从「赌神」手中接过泛着金属光泽的碎片，看起来很普通，没有任何值得说明的地方。出于乐善好施的崇高精神，你还是将机械零件塞进了口袋里。
t_en: You received a fragment from the "Ace Gambler" that shines with a metallic luster. It seems completely unremarkable, there is not much to be said about it. As you are a charitable and altruistic soul, you place the fragment into your pocket.

t_ch: 就当是个吉祥物也好嘛，赌神的手指，很珍贵的。信我的，不会错。
t_en: Take it as a lucky mascot, the finger of the Ace Gambler is very precious. Trust me, you can't go wrong.

t_ch: 要是实在缺钱了，就带着它去找<color=#dbc291ff>「斐迪南德」</color>，他在黄金的时刻，是个废零件商人。看到这个，他会给你开个好价钱的。
t_en: If you are ever low on money, take it to <color=#dbc291ff>Ferdinand</color> in the Golden Hour, he's a scrap parts dealer. He'll give you a good price when he sees this.

t_ch: 好了。最后的赌局就在那台扭蛋机前，一定来看啊。我等你。
t_en: Alright, the last gamble is over there on that random-item machine, you must come and watch. I'll be waiting.

t_ch: 抱歉…我不想牵扯进来。
t_en: Sorry... I don't want to be roped into this.

t_ch: 怎么回事，胆子这么小？哎，算了算了，随你吧，看来这回是天要亡我……
t_en: What? Are you that much of a coward? Ah, forget it, do what you want. I guess my demise is written in stone this time...

t_ch: 没什么事就快点走。这里巡逻的人多，别被看到了。自己多保重。
t_en: If you've got nothing else to do 'round here then get going. There are a lot of patrols round here, we don't want you being seen. Take care of yourself.

t_ch: 没什么事就快点走。这里巡逻的人多，别被看到了。自己多保重。
t_en: If you've got nothing else to do 'round here then get going. There are a lot of patrols round here, we don't want you being seen. Take care of yourself.

t_ch: 喂，你怎么看？
t_en: Hey, what do you think?

t_ch: 形势很焦灼，不好说。
t_en: It's pretty intense, it's hard to call.

t_ch: 这…赌神要赢了吧？
t_en: Well... Is the Ace Gambler going to win?

t_ch: 再来一个就好。
t_en: Just one more...

t_ch: 就差一个…
t_en: He only needs one more...

t_ch: 老大，加油啊…
t_en: Boss, you can do it...

t_ch: 不能输给这个宾客！
t_en: You can't lose to a guest!

t_ch: （好紧张的样子，还是先在旁边围观吧。）
t_en: (It looks pretty tense over here, maybe I should just watch from the sidelines for now.)

t_ch: …三同色。是你输了。
t_en: ...Three of the same symbol. You lose.

t_ch: 怎、怎么可能…怎么会有人连续抽出五次三同色的，这根本就是不可能的概率！
t_en: H—How is that possible... How can someone get three of the same symbol five times in a row? Those odds are basically impossible!

t_ch: 别说五次了，就算五十次我都能给你抽出来。碰上我是你的不幸。
t_en: Just five? Give me a chance and I'll pull it off fifty times for you! You were unfortunate to come up against me.

t_ch: 好了，愿赌服输。我要的东西，该乖乖交出来了。
t_en: Alright, time to pay up. Be good and hand it over.

t_ch: 跟我来，就你自己，不准带武器。哼，敢来就还给你。
t_en: Come with me. Alone, no weapons. Hmph, if you've got the guts, I'll give you what you want.

t_ch: 我倒要看看…所谓的赌神是否真的像传言里那样无所不能，谁都赢不过他！
t_en: I want to see if this so-called Ace Gambler really is as all-powerful as the legends say, let's see if you really can beat anyone!

t_ch: 行啊。你带路。
t_en: Fine. Lead the way.

t_ch: 灰色的朋友——你来了啊。上次真是要感谢你的帮助，要是还有机会，我会请你喝苏乐达。
t_en: My gray friend, you came! Thank you so much for your help before. If I get a chance, the SoulGlad is on me.

t_ch: 我还有事要办，就不多跟你闲聊了。保重。祝你好运。
t_en: I have other business to take care of, so I'll cut the pleasantries. Take care. Good luck.

t_ch: 你认识那位赌神吗？
t_en: Do you know this Ace Gambler?

t_ch: 啊，奥洛是我的老朋友，前段时间也在匹诺康尼。怎么了，你见过他吗？
t_en: Yeah, Oro was my old friend, he was in Penacony recently. Why, did you meet him?

t_ch: 他给了我这个。
t_en: He gave me this.

t_ch: 你将那块金属碎片递给了他。很显然，面前的老人认识这件物品。他反复摩挲着手中的零件，轻声的叹息恍如整个琥珀纪那般漫长。
t_en: You hand the metal fragment over. Evidently, the old man in front of you is familiar with this object. He runs the part through his hands repeatedly as he lets out a sigh as long as the Amber Era itself.

t_ch: …奥洛看人的眼光不会有错。还好你始终带着这块零件，并如约前往了现场——否则他就要输掉那场赌局了。
t_en: ...Oro is a great judge of character. It is fortunate that you held on to this fragment and went to watch the gamble as promised... Otherwise, he would have lost.

t_ch: 你看，顶端实际是中空的，里面藏有极微型的发信器。奥洛能在扭蛋机上百战百胜，靠的就是这个，他从远处就能操控得到的图案。
t_en: Look, the tip is completely hollow, with minuscule transmitters inside. The reason that Oro could play that random-item machine and win every time is all thanks to this. He was able to control which pattern he received from a distance.

t_ch: 家族当然不是好骗的，所以他把这个托付给了你，只要你能到场，他就有把握赢下这场和家族的生死局。他为这天准备了很久了。
t_en: Of course, The Family is not so easy to trick, so he gave this to you to look after. As long as you went to watch the game, he would be able to seize victory and win this gamble to the death with The Family. He spent a long time preparing for that day.

t_ch: 他得到想要的东西了吗？
t_en: Did he get what he wanted in the end?

t_ch: …那他现在呢？
t_en: ...Where is he now?

t_ch: 有段时间没听到奥洛的消息了。那天的赌局结束后，他就从梦境里消失了。没人知道他去了哪里，也没人知道他是不是还活着。
t_en: I have not heard any news of Oro for a while. After the gamble ended on that day, he disappeared from the dreamscape completely. No one knows where he went, no one knows if he is even still alive.

t_ch: 即使赢了赌局，家族也未必会服输。很多事情都没法放到桌上来说…奥洛心里明白得很。但为了拿回被夺走的那些东西，他别无选择。
t_en: Even if he did win the gamble, it does not mean that The Family admitted defeat. There were many things going on behind the scenes... Oro knew this all too well. However, he had no choice if he wanted to get back the things they had taken from him.

t_ch: 我知道的就这么多。那块碎片…既然他送给了你，就还是由你保管吧。
t_en: This is all I know. As for this fragment... He gave it to you, so you should keep it safe.

t_ch: 别再跟人提起他的事，对你没有好处。
t_en: Don't mention anything about him to anyone again, it will only cause you trouble.

t_ch: 啊…难得相识一场，还是送你份礼物吧。不是什么贵重品，但意义丰富。
t_en: Ah... It's rare to meet a friend of his, take this gift. It's nothing of value, but it has a lot of meaning.

t_ch: 请问你认识那位赌神吗？
t_en: Excuse me, do you know the Ace Gambler?

t_ch: 啊，奥洛是我的老朋友…有段时间没听到他的消息了。
t_en: Yeah, he was my old friend... I haven't heard anything from him in some time.

t_ch: 那天的赌局结束后，他就从梦境里消失了。我不知道他去了哪里。抱歉。
t_en: After that gamble ended, he disappeared from the dreamscape. I don't know where he went, sorry.

t_ch: （好紧张的样子，还是先在旁边围观吧。）
t_en: (It looks pretty tense over here, maybe I should just watch from the sidelines for now.)

t_ch: …不是同色。我输了。
t_en: ...It's not the same symbol. I lost.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，就这水平还自称赌神？我看你不如改名叫玩蛇吧！
t_en: Hahaha! Is that all that the Ace Gambler has to offer? Why not change your name to Fool of Fools!

t_ch: 用来作弊的那根手指被拿走后，你就没辙了？没想到我会在开局前检查你的部件吧？哼，别以为是智械就可以瞒天过海！
t_en: Lost your edge now that cheating hand of yours has been taken? Did you not think we'd check your parts before we started the bet? Hah! Don't go thinking you can fool everyone just because you're an Intellitron!

t_ch: 愿赌服输。按照约定，你的命现在归我了。把他带走！
t_en: Time to pay up. As we agreed, your life is mine. Take him away!

t_ch: …看来我的运气就到此为止了。
t_en: ...Looks like this is as far as my luck goes...

t_ch: 灰色的朋友，你来了。哎呀…运气不好，又让你看到了我狼狈的样子哈哈。都忘了吧。
t_en: My gray friend, you came. Ah... My luck ran out, now you have to see me in another sorry state, haha. Forget it...

t_ch: 这块碎片是…
t_en: What is this fragment...

t_ch: 在斐迪南德的废弃零件堆中，你发现了某个似曾相识的物品。
t_en: You discover an item inside Ferdinand's scrap metal heap that seems somehow familiar.

t_ch: 那是截断掉的金属零件，泛出的冷光让你心生寒意。你记得它曾经属于那位被称为「赌神」的智械宾客，而那场赌局后，你再也没见过他。
t_en: It is a severed metal fragment, the metallic shimmer it gives off sends a cold chill down your spine. You remember that it once belonged to the Intellitron visitor known as the Ace Gambler, but after the gamble it made on that fateful day, you never saw him again.

t_ch: 啊，奥洛是我的老朋友，前段时间也在匹诺康尼。怎么了，你见过他吗？
t_en: Yeah, Oro was my old friend, he was in Penacony recently. Why, did you meet him?

t_ch: 他在最后的赌局中落败了。
t_en: He lost his final gamble.

t_ch: 我和他曾有过短暂的接触。
t_en: We met briefly.

t_ch: 这样啊…感觉是个很有趣的故事，可惜和我没什么关系。
t_en: I see... I imagine it's quite an interesting story, though it is none of my business.

t_ch: 奥洛有他的目标，愿意为之付出所有，那就随他去吧。这样也好，总比在美梦里醉生梦死的那些人要强…呵，是比我强多了。
t_en: Oro had his intentions, if he was willing to give everything for them, then so be it. It's not a bad way to go, he's a stronger man than those who spend their lives chasing their dreams in a haze... Ah, he's much stronger than me.

t_ch: 听我一句劝，不要跟别人再提起他的事了——和他扯上关系对你没有好处。
t_en: Let me ask you one thing, though — do not mention him to anyone again, ever. Associating with him will only bring you harm.

t_ch: 难得相识一场，还是送你份礼物吧。不是什么贵重品，但意义丰富。
t_en: It's rare to meet a friend of his, take this gift. It's nothing of value, but it has a lot of meaning.

t_ch: 请问你认识那位赌神吗？
t_en: Excuse me, do you know the Ace Gambler?

t_ch: 啊，奥洛是我的老朋友…有段时间没听到他的消息了。
t_en: Yeah, he was my old friend... I haven't heard anything from him in some time.

t_ch: 都过去了，和我也没什么关系。往事就别再提了吧。
t_en: It's all in the past now and it's none of my business. Just leave the past in the past.

t_ch: 喂，你怎么看？
t_en: Hey, what do you think?

t_ch: 形势很焦灼，不好说。
t_en: It's pretty intense, it's hard to call.

t_ch: 这…赌神要输了吧？
t_en: Well... Is the Ace Gambler going to lose?

t_ch: 再来一个就好。
t_en: Just one more...

t_ch: 就差一个…
t_en: He only needs one more...

t_ch: 老大，加油啊…
t_en: Boss, you can do it...

t_ch: 让这群宾客尝尝苦头！
t_en: Give these visitors a taste of suffering!

t_ch: 喂，你看那边…
t_en: Hey, look over there...

t_ch: 不会真是他吧…
t_en: That can't really be him...

t_ch: 传说中赢遍所有星系。
t_en: They say he's won across every system...

t_ch: 出道数十年未尝败绩。
t_en: He's been undefeated for decades...

t_ch: 人尽皆知的…
t_en: Everyone knows him...

t_ch: …赌神！
t_en: ...The Ace Gambler!

t_ch: 这个人一直在赢钱。
t_en: This guy just keeps winning.

t_ch: 莫非是作弊？
t_en: Is he cheating?

t_ch: 不对劲…要去报告。
t_en: Something's not right... I have to report this.

t_ch: 这个人来历不简单。
t_en: This guy's something else.

t_ch: 得盯紧点…
t_en: We need to keep an eye on him...

t_ch: 我看他赢了好久了。
t_en: He's been winning like this for ages!

t_ch: 那台机器很走运啊…
t_en: This machine must be lucky...

t_ch: 你也快去试试！
t_en: Go and try it!

t_ch: 嘘。小点声。
t_en: Shh. Quiet.

t_ch: 那个人可惹不起…
t_en: You don't want to get on his bad side...

t_ch: 我刚才在筑梦边境…
t_en: You know, I just went to the Dream's Edge...

t_ch: 见到了昨天那个赌神！
t_en: And saw the Ace Gambler there yesterday!

t_ch: 你瞎编的吧？
t_en: Hey, you made that up, didn't you?

t_ch: 赌神跑那地方去干嘛。
t_en: Why would the Ace Gambler go to that place?

t_ch: 嗨，谁知道呢。
t_en: Well, who knows?

t_ch: 这些事还是少管为好。
t_en: Whatever it is, it's best to stay out of it.

t_ch: 听说赌神出现在这，是来找家族算账的！
t_en: I heard the Ace Gambler is here to settle a score with The Family!

t_ch: 不是吧，这么俗套的故事你也信？再说那个人是不是赌神，还不一定呢。
t_en: C'mon! How can you believe such a clichéd story? Besides, you don't know if he is actually the "Ace Gambler."

t_ch: 反正我是在<color=#dbc291ff>筑梦边境</color>看到他了，后面还跟着好几个猎犬家系的人。
t_en: Anyway, I saw him at the <color=#dbc291ff>Dream's Edge</color>, followed by some Bloodhounds.

t_ch: 哎，安心玩你的。这事也轮不到咱们掺和。
t_en: Just stay out of it. It's none of our business.

t_ch: …不知贵客长久驻足，究竟所为何事？
t_en: ...Is there a reason that you have been stopped in your tracks, dear guest?

t_ch: 若是听这小曲，心里头实在喜欢，不妨留点赏钱，小女子也好唱得更卖力些。
t_en: If this song of mine brings you joy, perhaps you could leave a small tip and I will put even more heart into my performance.

t_ch: 只想寻个消遣也无妨。您是自由身，行动念想可都不必受限于人。
t_en: If you are merely passing the time, that is no problem. You are a free soul, your will need not be limited by anyone.

t_ch: 怎叹…唉。
t_en: Ahh...

t_ch: 遇到什么困难了吗？
t_en: Are you in some kind of trouble?

t_ch: 你似乎话里有话啊。
t_en: I feel like you want to talk about something?

t_ch: 还是免了吧。家母在的时候曾教导过，随意提起自己的难处，对贵客未免太过不敬重。
t_en: I probably should not talk about it. She taught me that it would be disrespectful of me to speak of my troubles to guests.

t_ch: 我虽孤身流落异乡，所幸还有这弹词唱曲的本事，谋得少许现钞，挣得几声喝彩，不至于生活没个着落，就不烦扰您了。
t_en: Though I am alone in a faraway land, I am able to sing and perform — to earn some loose changes and applause. It's not like I have nothing, so I shall not bother you.

t_ch: 缘何起…望何期…多别离……
t_en: Oh why... When shall we meet again... So many partings...

t_ch: 跟我说说你的事吧。
t_en: You can talk to me.

t_ch: 好吧，那你多保重。
t_en: Okay, take care.

t_ch: 唉。想必您也看出来了，我并非这儿的本地人，沦落至此…全因过往的那场悲剧。
t_en: Ah. I'm sure you can tell, I am not of this place. My being here is all because of that tragedy in the past...

t_ch: 某次星际航行途中，我们的舰船遭遇了极为凶险的蛰虫群袭击。我虽侥幸躲过劫难，家父与家母却身躯残破，伤重昏迷，最终仍旧不治……
t_en: During an interstellar voyage, our ship was attacked by a swarm of ferocious stinging insects. I was fortunate enough to escape disaster, but my mother and father were maimed and sustained severe injuries. They collapsed from their heavy wounds and soon passed away...

t_ch: 为了替他们寻得妥善的回收场地，不至于落得遭人拆解倒卖的下场，我不得不四处筹措资金，最终欠下巨款，被迫签订了永久的依附合约。
t_en: I had to search all around and raise funds so that I could find a suitable recycling site for them, so they would not be broken down and resold. In the end, I racked up such a massive debt that I was compelled to sign a contract of subservience for perpetuity.

t_ch: 我的余生都将在无止境的劳作中度过，供人娱乐，乞人怜悯，徘徊…漫无归处。
t_en: I am to spend the rest of my life working endlessly, entertaining people, wandering as a pitiful beggar... with no place to call home.

t_ch: …确实很可怜啊。
t_en: ...That is really sad.

t_ch: 智械…也有父母？
t_en: Intellitrons... have parents, too?

t_ch: 不好意思，这是我擅自的称呼。让您误解了。
t_en: Sorry, I just called them that, sorry for the misunderstanding.

t_ch: 事实上，他们是始终陪伴我的两位长辈。我们共同生活了许久，关系极为亲密。
t_en: They are actually two elders that were always by my side. We lived together for so long, we became extremely close.

t_ch: 我没法接受他们的残躯散落各处，为此用尽了全部积蓄，直至负债累累。
t_en: I could not accept their bodies being scattered all over, so I spent all of my savings and racked up massive debt.

t_ch: 抱歉，我还有事。
t_en: Sorry, I have other things to do.

t_ch: 要是这些无聊的闲话，使您心生怜意的话……
t_en: If this pathetic story of mine has roused just a little pity in your heart...

t_ch: 我在此想…冒昧地提出请求，要是您手头碰巧有多余的闲置资金，是否可以考虑……
t_en: Perhaps... this is a bold request but, I don't suppose that you happen to have a little spare change that you would consider using to...

t_ch: 为我…赎回自由呢？
t_en: Help me... buy back my freedom?

t_ch: 小女子深知自己本是劳碌命，不求坐享荣华富贵，但若是此生有幸，得以服侍如您这般…懂我、关照我的贵客，也算是有个安稳的去处了。
t_en: I know that I am fated to a life of labor, I dream not of a life of riches and indulgence. It's just, if I were fortunate enough to serve a guest... a guest like you, who understands me and cares for me, that would be enough of a stable home for me.

t_ch: 您…愿意接纳我，允我伴在身边么？
t_en: Will... Will you accept me and allow me to remain by your side?

t_ch: …我愿意。
t_en: ...I will.

t_ch: 我不愿意。
t_en: I will not.

t_ch: 我目前需偿还的债款是…两万信用点。先在此谢过了。
t_en: The debt that I need to pay now is... twenty thousand credits. Thank you in advance.

t_ch: 我全帮你还了。
t_en: I will clear that for you.

t_ch: 您的慷慨仗义，小女子将永远铭记于心。
t_en: I shall remember your generosity and kindness in my heart for eternity.

t_ch: 请允许我先行离开去与同伴作别。稍后与您在此会面。
t_en: Please allow me to first say farewell to my companions. I shall meet you here again shortly.

t_ch: 对不起，我没那么多钱。
t_en: Sorry, I don't have that much money.

t_ch: 您无需自责。是我不该提出如此无礼的请求。
t_en: You must not blame yourself. It was me that should not have made such a rude request.

t_ch: 若是不嫌弃的话，可以时常来此处逛逛，听小女子唱个曲，也算是为您解闷了。
t_en: If you would like, please come back for a stroll and to enjoy my singing. I would like to at least relieve your boredom.

t_ch: 多别离…无人怜惜…空悲戚……
t_en: So many partings... No one to pity... Full of sorrow...

t_ch: 还有什么能为你做的吗？
t_en: Is there anything else I can do for you?

t_ch: 如果…您真想为我做点什么，那能不能…请您带我从这逃走呢？
t_en: If... If you really wish to do something for me, could you... could you help me escape from here?

t_ch: 我已经受够了任人摆布的生活，若是有机会重获自由，我愿付出全部代价。
t_en: I have had enough of living under the whims of others, if I have a chance to take back my freedom, I would pay any price.

t_ch: 小女子…在此诚心恳求您。
t_en: I am... I am begging you.

t_ch: 我会帮你。
t_en: I will help you.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>筑梦边境</color>…我在那等着您。
t_en: I shall await you at the <color=#dbc291ff>Dream's Edge</color>...

t_ch: 这件事太危险了…
t_en: That is too dangerous...

t_ch: 是吧。我确实不该将旁人拖进来，请您…还是都忘了吧。
t_en: You're right. I shouldn't be dragging others into this. Please... just forget what I said.

t_ch: 小女子重获自由身，全仰仗您的善意。
t_en: Thanks to your kindness, I have finally reclaimed my freedom.

t_ch: 大恩不言谢。如今依附合约已解除，我也没个好去处，往后该如何安置，听凭您差遣。
t_en: Words will never be enough to describe my gratitude. Now that the contract of subservience is broken, I have nowhere to go. I shall leave the decision of my future in your hands.

t_ch: 只是…有件事始终萦绕在心中，也不知当不当讲……
t_en: It's just... There is something that still weighs on my chest, I am uncertain whether I should tell you...

t_ch: 你先说吧。
t_en: What exactly do you need?

t_ch: 怎么话这么多呢。
t_en: You've sure got a lot to say.

t_ch: 在先前的落脚处，小女子曾遇到许多…和我有着相同遭遇的兄弟姐妹们，他们同样身世悲惨，或是家遭不幸，或是患有重疾，生活困苦，难以为继。
t_en: In the place where I was kept, I met many... many brothers and sisters who had shared my experiences. They had encountered tragedy, their families had met with misfortune, or they were stricken with illness. Their lives were hard, and they struggled to make a living.

t_ch: 我与他们相处甚久，早已是一心同体。实在不忍独自在外逍遥，却留同伴仍在此受苦。
t_en: I've been with them for so long, we've become inseparable. I cannot bear to be so far away while I know my companions are suffering in this place.

t_ch: 要是您有余力，愿意继续大发善心的话…可否请您，也替他们赎回自由呢？
t_en: If you are willing to express your endless generosity... perhaps you would also pay for their freedom on their behalf?

t_ch: 大家都是懂得知恩图报的人，不会让您的好意白费的。
t_en: They are all people who understand the meaning of repaying kindness, your gesture would not go to waste.

t_ch: …我愿意。
t_en: ...I will.

t_ch: 他们目前需偿还的债款是…六万四千八百信用点。先在此谢过了。
t_en: The amount that they need to repay is... sixty-four thousand and eight hundred credits. Thank you in advance.

t_ch: 我全付了！
t_en: All shall be cleared!

t_ch: 您的慷慨与仗义，小女子将永远铭记于心。
t_en: I shall remember your generosity and kindness in my heart for eternity.

t_ch: 请允许我先行离开去通知各位兄弟姐妹。稍后与您在此会面。
t_en: Please allow me to first inform my siblings. I shall meet you here again shortly.

t_ch: 为您特意增设的盛典即将召开…还请务必出席。
t_en: A grand ceremony dedicated to you is about to begin... Please make sure you do not miss it.

t_ch: 对不起，我没那么多钱。
t_en: Sorry, I don't have that much money.

t_ch: 您无需自责，是我不该提出如此无礼的请求。就当小女子没说过吧。
t_en: You must not blame yourself. It was me that should not have made such a rude request. Pretend I never said anything.

t_ch: 离开前，能否请您带我去梦境各处逛逛，呆得久了，临走时竟还有些舍不得。
t_en: Before we leave, can I ask you to take me to walk around the dreamscape? I have been here so long now, I am almost a little reluctant to leave.

t_ch: 就从<color=#dbc291ff>筑梦边境</color>开始吧。听说那儿的风景很独特，但不对宾客开放，只能拜托您偷偷带我过去了。
t_en: Let's start with the <color=#dbc291ff>Dream's Edge</color>. I hear that the scenery there is like no other, but it is not open to guests, I will have to ask you to sneak me in.

t_ch: 我不愿意。
t_en: I will not.

t_ch: 您无需自责，是我不该提出如此无礼的请求。就当小女子没说过吧。
t_en: You must not blame yourself. It was me that should not have made such a rude request. Pretend I never said anything.

t_ch: 离开前，能否请您带我去梦境各处逛逛，呆得久了，临走时竟还有些舍不得。
t_en: Before we leave, can I ask you to take me to walk around the dreamscape? I have been here so long now, I am almost a little reluctant to leave.

t_ch: 就从<color=#dbc291ff>筑梦边境</color>开始吧。听说那儿的风景很独特，但不对宾客开放，只能拜托您偷偷带我过去了。
t_en: Let's start with the <color=#dbc291ff>Dream's Edge</color>. I hear that the scenery there is like no other, but it is not open to guests, I will have to ask you to sneak me in.

t_ch: …小女子在这等了许久，还以为您不会来了呢。
t_en: ...I have been waiting here for so long. I thought you would not come.

t_ch: 快些走吧。当心那群人追上来。要是真遇到什么麻烦事，也还得仰仗您……
t_en: Let us walk faster. I don't want them to catch us. If we get into trouble, I also have to rely on you...

t_ch: 那群人是…？
t_en: "They"? Who are "they"...?

t_ch: 是猎犬家系。我的依附合约…就是和他们签订的。
t_en: The Bloodhound Family. My contract of subservience... was signed with them.

t_ch: 我会保护你的。
t_en: I will protect you.

t_ch: 这种显而易见的事，小女子自然明白。
t_en: That is obvious. I know that.

t_ch: 你先前可没说还有这事啊。
t_en: You didn't tell me about that earlier.

t_ch: 但您应该也大致猜到了吧，否则…小女子又何必恳求您呢。
t_en: But you must have had an idea, right? Otherwise... why would I have begged you?

t_ch: 前面那座拱门，您瞧见了吧。越过那里就暂时安全了，希望…途中不要出事。
t_en: You see the arch door up ahead, right? Once we pass through, we will be safe for now. I hope... nothing happens before we get there.

t_ch: ——你们！给我站住！单方面违约必须承担赔偿责任，不偿还债款就想跑？
t_en: You! Stop right there! A unilateral breach of contract means you must pay for damages! You think you can run off without settling your debt?

t_ch: （出现得是不是有点太巧了？）
t_en: (That timing is a little too coincidental, isn't it?)

t_ch: 惜叹…好事难成啊。
t_en: *sigh* I knew it was too good to be true.

t_ch: 哼。人已经到手了，就到此为止吧。兄弟们，把这个逃走的女人带回去！
t_en: Hah, we've secured her, we can leave it here. Men, take this fugitive back where she belongs!

t_ch: 对不起，我不能再给您添更多麻烦了。
t_en: Sorry, I cannot cause you any more trouble.

t_ch: 请您…忘了我吧。
t_en: Please just... forget me.

t_ch: （没有看到浔桑，这是…她留下的字条？）
t_en: (You cannot see Xunsang anywhere. Is this... a note that she left you?)

t_ch: 纸上写着：「想了许久，还是不愿再给您添麻烦了。从今往后，我将珍惜您给予的、来之不易的自由，独立坚强地活下去。衷心地…祝您幸福。」
t_en: The note says: "I thought for a long time and decided not to add to your troubles. From today onwards I will treasure the hard-won freedom that you have gifted me. I will stand on my two feet. I truly... wish you happiness."

t_ch: （她应该是不会来了，回去吧。）
t_en: (She is not coming. You should head back.)

t_ch: …小女子在此恭候多时了。
t_en: ...I have been waiting for you.

t_ch: 有个好消息要与您分享。鉴于…您是本店尊贵的第八百八十八万位客人，此前的消费金额将悉数返还到您的账户，请您注意查收。
t_en: I have wonderful news to share with you. As you are the <unbreak>8,880,000</unbreak>th customer in our store, all previous purchases you have made will be refunded to your account. Please make sure you receive your funds.

t_ch: 盛典的筹办已经完成，随时可以进行。您做好准备了吗？
t_en: The preparations for your grand ceremony are complete and we can proceed at any time. Are you prepared?

t_ch: 可以开始了。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: …准备？什么准备？
t_en: ...Prepared? Prepared for what?

t_ch: 当然是…您花费重金点选的「帝皇荣光服务专享卡（多人豪华版）」。
t_en: For the Imperial Glory Exclusive Service Card (Multi-Party Luxury Version) that you paid a large sum of money for, of course!

t_ch: 时长有限，请尽兴享用。拍照上传到社交平台并撰写五星好评，还可获得大额满减优惠券哦。
t_en: The service runs for a limited time, so please enjoy it while you can. Share a picture on social media and leave a 5-star review to receive high-value discount coupons.

t_ch: …不知贵客长久驻足，究竟所为何事？
t_en: ...Is there a reason that you have been stopped in your tracks, dear guest?

t_ch: 若是听这小曲，心里头实在喜欢，不妨留点打赏，小女子也好唱得更卖力些。
t_en: If this song of mine brings you joy, perhaps you could leave a small tip and I will put even more heart into my performance.

t_ch: 只想寻个消遣也无妨。您是自由身，行动念想可都不必受限于人。
t_en: If you are merely passing the time, that is no problem. You are a free soul, your will need not be limited by anyone.

t_ch: 怎叹…唉。
t_en: Ahh...

t_ch: …你没事吧？
t_en: ...Are you okay?

t_ch: 还记得我吗？
t_en: Do you remember me?

t_ch: 小女子似乎与您…未曾相识？
t_en: I don't believe... I've met you before?

t_ch: 最近这批智械的芯片出了些存储故障，历史数据都丢了。抱歉抱歉。
t_en: Recently, there has been a storage malfunction with this batch of chips for Intellitrons. We've lost all the historical data. Sorry about that.

t_ch: 哎呀，我记得您。您就是上次游玩「逃跑情境体验卡（免费版）」的客人吧！
t_en: Ah, I remember you. You must be the customer who played the free version of the Escape Simulation Experience Card!

t_ch: 感觉还行？这可是我们最近新推出的沉浸式服务——真实场景，自由选择，丰富互动，还有超高智能的实力演员，包您能完美代入！
t_en: How was it? This is our newly launched immersive experience — featuring realistic scenes, freedom of choice, rich interaction, and intelligent and skilled actors. Your perfect immersion is guaranteed!

t_ch: 难道她…只是在演戏？
t_en: Could she just be... acting?

t_ch: 当然啊。哼哼…看您的样子，怕不是入戏太深，真的动了感情？
t_en: Of course. Hohoho... Look at you. You were so into the act... Did you develop some real feelings?

t_ch: 哎呦，大可不必。就当是看个戏，戏中人戏外身，可得好好分清楚才行。
t_en: Oh dear, don't be. Just think of it as watching a play. You'll have to differentiate between the character in the play and their real self.

t_ch: 不过这也说明…您很满意对吧？行嘞，您喜欢就好！
t_en: But that also means... you're satisfied with it, right? Well, as long as you like it!

t_ch: 果然，我早就猜到了。
t_en: Sure enough, I guessed it.

t_ch: 就是演个戏嘛。情景和台词都有不少欠打磨的地方，别太较真哈。
t_en: It's just an act. There are still a few scenes and lines that need polishing, so don't take it too seriously.

t_ch: 不过您都看到了最后，应该很满意对吧？行嘞，您喜欢就好！
t_en: But since you've stayed till the end, you should be satisfied, right? Well, as long as you like it!

t_ch: 欢迎下次再来体验*豪华版*的内容啊。
t_en: You're welcome to come back to experience the *deluxe edition* of our show!

t_ch: …不知贵客长久驻足，究竟所为何事？
t_en: ...Is there a reason that you have been stopped in your tracks, dear guest?

t_ch: 若是听这小曲，心里头实在喜欢，不妨留点打赏，小女子也好唱得更卖力些。
t_en: If this song of mine brings you joy, perhaps you could leave a small tip and I will put even more heart into my performance.

t_ch: 只想寻个消遣也无妨。您是自由身，行动念想可都不必受限于人。
t_en: If you are merely passing the time, that is no problem. You are a free soul, your will need not be limited by anyone.

t_ch: 怎叹…唉。
t_en: Ahh...

t_ch: 等等…你怎么回来了？！
t_en: Hold up... Why are you back!?

t_ch: 还记得我吗？
t_en: Do you remember me?
